steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Besterman and Pace hypothesized that macroalgae negatively affect MPB biomass. Their actual result: no significant correlation between benthic chlorophyll and macroalgal mat abundance. The blank must draw a conclusion that follows logically from that null result.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'their hypothesis about the relationship between macroalgae and MPB was not supported and their finding was instead consistent with that of Hardison and colleagues': No correlation means no negative effect — the hypothesis of a negative relationship is not supported. This also aligns with Hardison and colleagues, who similarly found macroalgae do not impact MPB. Both conditions are met. ✓
  • Choice B — 'although this finding was consistent with that of García-Robledo and Corzo, trends in MPB biomass are likely not a result of stresses caused by macroalgae': Gets the comparison backwards. García-Robledo and Corzo found that benthic chlorophyll decreased with macroalgae — a negative relationship. Besterman and Pace found no correlation, which contradicts García-Robledo and Corzo, not confirms it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'differences between their study design and that of García-Robledo and Corzo likely explain the discrepancy in the two studies’ findings': Invents an explanation for the discrepancy. The passage provides no information about study design differences; this inference is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice D — 'rather than inhibiting chlorophyll production in MPB, macroalgal mats may positively affect chlorophyll production': No correlation means no relationship in either direction. Inferring a positive effect from a null result is an unwarranted leap. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
